title: Hymenasplenium subnormale
intro: Hymenasplenium subnormale, also known as subnormal spleenwort, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Aspleniaceae. Traditionally, Hymenasplenium subnormale has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hymenasplenium subnormal is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine as a component of various herbal formulas to treat respiratory and digestive issues.
